sentences.chinese: 季布，楚人也，為任俠有名。
sentences.literal: Ji Bu, a man of Chu, as one who practiced knight-errantry had a name.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was a native of Chu and was widely known as a knight-errant.

sentences.chinese: 項籍使將兵，數窘漢王。
sentences.literal: Xiang Ji sent him to command troops; repeatedly embarrassed the King of Han.
sentences.idiomatic: Xiang Yu sent him to lead troops, and time and again he drove the King of Han into desperate straits.

sentences.chinese: 項籍滅，高祖購求布千金，敢有舍匿，罪三族。
sentences.literal: Xiang Ji destroyed; Gaozu offered a thousand in gold to seek Bu; whoever dared harbor or conceal him, crime to three clans.
sentences.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu fell, Gaozu offered a thousand catties of gold for Ji Bu's capture, and anyone who dared shelter or hide him would be punished with extermination of three generations of his kin.

sentences.chinese: 布匿濮陽周氏，周氏曰：「漢求將軍急，跡且至臣家，能聽臣，臣敢進計；
sentences.literal: Bu hid with the Zhou clan of Puyang. The Zhou said: "The Han seeks the general urgently; tracks will soon reach my house. If you can heed your subject, your subject dares advance a plan;"
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu took refuge with the Zhou family of Puyang. The head of the family said, "The Han court is hunting you with great urgency, and their agents will soon trace you here. If you will listen to me, I venture to propose a plan;"

sentences.chinese: 即否，願先自剄。」
sentences.literal: If not, I wish first myself to cut the throat."
sentences.idiomatic: If not, I would rather cut my own throat first."

sentences.chinese: 布許之。
sentences.literal: Bu assented to it.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu agreed.

sentences.chinese: 乃髡鉗布，衣褐，置廣柳車中，并與其家僮數十人，之魯朱家所賣之。
sentences.literal: Then shaved-head and cangue Bu, clothed him in coarse cloth, placed in a broad-willow cart, together with several tens of his household servants, went to Lu to Zhu Jia's place and sold him.
sentences.idiomatic: They shaved Ji Bu's head, fitted him with an iron collar, dressed him in a laborer's garb, and stowed him in a covered funeral cart along with several dozen household retainers sold as bondservants, then took the party to Lu and sold them to Zhu Jia.

sentences.chinese: 朱家心知其季布也，買置田舍。
sentences.literal: Zhu Jia's heart knew him to be Ji Bu; bought and placed on fields and houses.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Jia knew at heart that the man was Ji Bu; he purchased him and settled him on a farm.

sentences.chinese: 乃之雒陽見汝陰侯滕公，說曰：「季布何罪？
sentences.literal: Then went to Luoyang, saw Marquis of Ru-yin Lord Teng, persuaded saying: "What crime has Ji Bu?"
sentences.idiomatic: He then traveled to Luoyang to see the Marquis of Ru-yin, Lord Teng (Xiahou Ying), and urged him: "What crime has Ji Bu committed?"

sentences.chinese: 臣各為其主用，職耳。
sentences.literal: Ministers each for their lord were employed; duty only.
sentences.idiomatic: Each minister served the lord he owed allegiance to; that was simply his duty.

sentences.chinese: 項氏臣豈可盡誅邪？
sentences.literal: Xiang clan's ministers how can all be executed?"
sentences.idiomatic: Surely you cannot put every minister who served the Xiangs to the sword?"

sentences.chinese: 今上始得天下，而以私怨求一人，何示不廣也！
sentences.literal: Now the sovereign has just obtained the realm, yet by private resentment seeks one man—how show not broad!"
sentences.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven has only just won the empire; to hunt one man out of private spite shows how narrow his magnanimity would appear!"

sentences.chinese: 且以季布之賢，漢求之急如此，此不北走胡，南走越耳。
sentences.literal: Moreover by Ji Bu's worthiness, Han's seeking him urgently thus—this is not north flee Hu, south flee Yue only.
sentences.idiomatic: Besides, a man of Ji Bu's caliber, pressed this hard by the Han, will flee north to the Hu or south to Yue—that is all there is to it.

sentences.chinese: 夫忌壯士以資敵國，此伍子胥所以鞭荊平之墓也。
sentences.literal: As for envying a stalwart to supply an enemy state—this is why Wu Zixu whipped King Ping of Jing's tomb."
sentences.idiomatic: To drive a brave man into the arms of a rival power out of jealousy is the very reason Wu Zixu lashed the grave of King Ping of Chu."

sentences.chinese: 君何不從容為上言之？」
sentences.literal: Why do you not at ease for the sovereign speak of it?"
sentences.idiomatic: Why do you not find a suitable moment and put this to the sovereign for him?"

sentences.chinese: 滕公心知朱家大俠，意布匿其所，乃許諾。
sentences.literal: Lord Teng's heart knew Zhu Jia was a great knight-errant; suspected Bu hid at his place; then promised.
sentences.idiomatic: Lord Teng knew Zhu Jia for a great knight-errant and guessed that Ji Bu lay hidden with him; he gave his word.

sentences.chinese: 侍間，果言如朱家指。
sentences.literal: Waiting for an interval, indeed spoke as Zhu Jia indicated.
sentences.idiomatic: When an opportunity arose, he spoke to the emperor exactly as Zhu Jia had directed.

sentences.chinese: 上乃赦布。
sentences.literal: The sovereign then pardoned Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon pardoned Ji Bu.

sentences.chinese: 當是時，諸公皆多布能摧剛為柔，朱家亦以此名聞當世。
sentences.literal: At this time, the great men all praised Bu able to grind hard into soft; Zhu Jia also by this had name heard in that age.
sentences.idiomatic: At that time eminent men alike admired Ji Bu for turning inflexible strength into yielding survival, and Zhu Jia's reputation spread through the age for the same deed.

sentences.chinese: 布召見，謝，拜郎中。
sentences.literal: Bu summoned and seen, thanked, appointed Gentleman of the Palace.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was summoned into audience, expressed his gratitude, and was appointed Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.chinese: 孝惠時，為中郎將。
sentences.literal: In Emperor Xiaohui's time, served as General of the Gentlemen of the Palace.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaohui he served as General of the Gentlemen of the Palace.

sentences.chinese: 單于嘗為書嫚呂太后，太后怒，召諸將議之。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu once made a letter insulting Empress Dowager Lü; the empress dowager angry, summoned the generals to deliberate on it.
sentences.idiomatic: The Chanyu had once sent a letter that insulted Empress Dowager Lü; enraged, she called in the generals to discuss a response.

sentences.chinese: 上將軍樊噲曰：「臣願得十萬眾，橫行匈奴中。」
sentences.literal: Senior General Fan Kuai said: "Your servant wishes to obtain a hundred thousand host, rampage through the Xiongnu middle."
sentences.idiomatic: Senior General Fan Kuai declared, "I ask for a hundred thousand men to sweep through the heart of the Xiongnu domain."

sentences.chinese: 諸將皆阿呂太后，以噲言為然。
sentences.literal: The generals all fawned on Empress Dowager Lü, took Kuai's words as right.
sentences.idiomatic: The generals all curried favor with Empress Dowager Lü and agreed that Fan Kuai was right.

sentences.chinese: 布曰：「樊噲可斬也。
sentences.literal: Bu said: "Fan Kuai can be beheaded.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu said, "Fan Kuai deserves execution.

sentences.chinese: 夫以高帝兵三十餘萬，困於平城，噲時亦在其中。
sentences.literal: As for with Gaodi's troops thirty-odd myriad, trapped at Pingcheng—Kuai at that time also was within them.
sentences.idiomatic: Gaodi led more than three hundred thousand men and was still trapped at Pingcheng, and Fan Kuai was in that army.

sentences.chinese: 今噲奈何以十萬眾橫行匈奴中，面謾！
sentences.literal: Now how can Kuai with a hundred thousand host rampage through Xiongnu middle—face to deceive!"
sentences.idiomatic: How dare Fan Kuai now claim he can ride roughshod over the Xiongnu with a mere hundred thousand—he insults us to our faces!"

sentences.chinese: 且秦以事胡，陳勝等起。
sentences.literal: Moreover Qin by serving the Hu, Chen Sheng and others rose.
sentences.idiomatic: Moreover, when Qin exhausted itself on the northern tribes, Chen Sheng and his like rose in rebellion.

sentences.chinese: 今瘡痍未瘳，噲又面諛，欲搖動天下。」
sentences.literal: Now wounds not healed; Kuai again face flatters, wishes to shake move the realm."
sentences.idiomatic: The wounds of war are not yet healed, and Fan Kuai flatters you to your face in a bid to unsettle the whole realm."

sentences.chinese: 是時殿上皆恐，太后罷朝，遂不復議擊匈奴事。
sentences.literal: At this time on the hall all feared; empress dowager dismissed court; then no more deliberated striking Xiongnu affairs.
sentences.idiomatic: A chill fell over everyone in the hall; the empress dowager ended the audience, and the court never again debated attacking the Xiongnu.

sentences.chinese: 布為河東守。
sentences.literal: Bu was Hedong governor.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu served as governor of Hedong.

sentences.chinese: 孝文時，人有言其賢，召欲以為御史大夫。
sentences.literal: In Emperor Xiaowen's time, someone spoke of his worthiness; summoned, wished to make him Grandee Secretary.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen, someone praised his abilities, and the emperor summoned him intending to appoint him Grandee Secretary.

sentences.chinese: 人又言其勇，使酒難近。
sentences.literal: Someone again spoke of his bravery, wine-driven hard to approach.
sentences.idiomatic: Others spoke of his courage but added that he was unruly when drunk and difficult to work with.

sentences.chinese: 至，留邸一月，見罷。
sentences.literal: Arrived, lodged in guesthouse one month, audience dismissed.
sentences.idiomatic: When he arrived, he was kept at the official lodging for a month and then dismissed without appointment.

sentences.chinese: 布進曰：「臣待罪河東，陛下無故召臣，此人必有以臣欺陛下者。
sentences.literal: Bu advanced saying: "Your servant awaits punishment at Hedong; Your Majesty without reason summoned your servant—this person must have something by which to deceive Your Majesty with your servant."
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu stepped forward and said, "I stand awaiting judgment in Hedong; Your Majesty summoned me without cause—someone must have misrepresented me to you."

sentences.chinese: 今臣至，無所受事，罷去，此人必有毀臣者。
sentences.literal: Now your servant arrived, received no affairs, dismissed and sent away—this person must have one who slanders your servant.
sentences.idiomatic: Now that I have come and been given no assignment but sent away, someone must have slandered me."

sentences.chinese: 夫陛下以一人譽召臣，一人毀去臣，臣恐天下有識者聞之，有以窺陛下。」
sentences.literal: As for Your Majesty by one man's praise summoned your servant, by one man's slander dismissed your servant—your servant fears the realm's knowing ones hearing it will have means to spy Your Majesty."
sentences.idiomatic: If a single man's praise can bring me to court and a single man's blame can send me away, I fear that discerning men everywhere will draw their own conclusions about Your Majesty's judgment."

sentences.chinese: 上默然，慚曰：「河東吾股肱郡，故特召君耳。」
sentences.literal: The sovereign silent, ashamed said: "Hedong is my thigh-arm province; therefore specially summoned you only."
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor was silent, then said with embarrassment, "Hedong is a pillar of the realm; that is why I called you here in particular."

sentences.chinese: 布之官。
sentences.literal: Bu went to his office.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu returned to his post.

sentences.chinese: 辯士曹丘生數招權顧金錢，事貴人趙談等，與竇長君善。
sentences.literal: Sophist Cao Qiusheng repeatedly summoned power looked to gold and cash, served noble men Zhao Tan and others, with Dou Changjun was good.
sentences.idiomatic: The rhetorician Cao Qiusheng often traded on influence for money, cultivated men such as the favorite Zhao Tan, and was on close terms with Dou Changjun.

sentences.chinese: 布聞，寄書諫長君曰：「吾聞曹丘生非長者，勿與通。」
sentences.literal: Bu heard, sent letter remonstrating Changjun saying: "I have heard Cao Qiusheng is not an elder-worthy; do not communicate with him."
sentences.idiomatic: When Ji Bu heard of it, he sent Dou Changjun a letter of warning: "I have heard that Cao Qiusheng is no man of honor; have nothing to do with him."

sentences.chinese: 及曹丘生歸，欲得書請布。
sentences.literal: When Cao Qiusheng returned, wished to obtain a letter to request Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng came home, he wanted a letter of introduction so he could call on Ji Bu.

sentences.chinese: 竇長君曰：「季將軍不說足下，足下無往。」
sentences.literal: Dou Changjun said: "General Ji is not pleased with you; you, do not go."
sentences.idiomatic: Dou Changjun said, "General Ji dislikes you; you had better not go."

sentences.chinese: 固請書，遂行。
sentences.literal: Firmly requested letter; then went.
sentences.idiomatic: Cao pressed for the letter until he got it, then set out.

sentences.chinese: 使人先發書，布果大怒，待曹丘。
sentences.literal: Sent man first to deliver letter; Bu indeed greatly angry, awaited Cao Qiu.
sentences.idiomatic: He sent someone ahead with the letter; Ji Bu was furious and waited for Cao Qiusheng in a rage.

sentences.chinese: 曹丘至，則揖布曰：「楚人諺曰『得黃金百，不如得季布諾』，足下何以得此聲梁楚之間哉？
sentences.literal: Cao Qiu arrived, then bowed to Bu saying: "Chu people proverb says 'obtain yellow gold hundred, not like obtain Ji Bu's promise'—how did you obtain this reputation between Liang and Chu?"
sentences.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng arrived, he bowed to Ji Bu and said, "The people of Chu have a saying: 'A hundred catties of gold are not worth Ji Bu's word.' How did you win such a name across Liang and Chu?"

sentences.chinese: 且僕與足下俱楚人，使僕游揚足下名於天下，顧不美乎？
sentences.literal: Moreover your servant and you both Chu men; if your servant roam-extol your foot's name in the realm—look, is it not beautiful?"
sentences.idiomatic: Moreover, you and I are both men of Chu; if I spread your reputation through the empire, would that not be splendid?"

sentences.chinese: 何足下距僕之深也！」
sentences.literal: Why you reject your servant so deeply!"
sentences.idiomatic: Why do you keep me at such a distance!"

sentences.chinese: 布乃大說。
sentences.literal: Bu then greatly pleased.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was greatly pleased.

sentences.chinese: 引入，留數月，為上客，厚送之。
sentences.literal: Led him in, retained several months, made upper guest, thickly sent him off.
sentences.idiomatic: He brought him inside, kept him for several months as an honored guest, and sent him away with rich gifts.

sentences.chinese: 布名所以益聞者，曹丘揚之也。
sentences.literal: Bu's name the reason it increased heard—Cao Qiu extolled it.
sentences.idiomatic: The reason Ji Bu's fame grew still wider was that Cao Qiusheng proclaimed it.

sentences.chinese: 布弟季心氣蓋關中，遇人恭謹，為任俠，方數千里，士爭為死。
sentences.literal: Bu's younger brother Ji Xin spirit covered Guanzhong; meeting people respectful cautious; as knight-errant, region several thousand li, knights vied to die for him.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu's younger brother Ji Xin's daring overshadowed the Guanzhong region; though courteous and restrained in personal dealings, as a knight-errant his reputation spread for thousands of li, and fighting men vied to die for him.

sentences.chinese: 嘗殺人，亡吳，從爰絲匿，長事爰絲，弟畜灌夫、籍福之屬。
sentences.literal: Once killed a man, fled to Wu, followed Yuan Si hiding, elder-served Yuan Si, younger-treated Guan Fu, Ji Fu's sort.
sentences.idiomatic: He once killed a man and fled to Wu, where he took refuge with Yuan Ang and served him with the deference due an elder, while treating Guan Fu, Ji Fu, and their circle as younger companions.

sentences.chinese: 嘗為中司馬，中尉郅都不敢加。
sentences.literal: Once was central marshal; Commandant Zhi Du did not dare apply pressure.
sentences.idiomatic: He had served as a marshal in the central command; even Commandant Zhi Du did not dare cross him.

sentences.chinese: 少年多時時竊借其名以行。
sentences.literal: Youths many often secretly borrowed his name to act.
sentences.idiomatic: Young men often quietly traded on his name to get their way.

sentences.chinese: 當是時，季心以勇，布以諾，聞關中。
sentences.literal: At this time, Ji Xin by courage, Bu by promise, heard in Guanzhong.
sentences.idiomatic: In those days Ji Xin was famed in Guanzhong for his bravery and Ji Bu for keeping his word.

sentences.chinese: 布母弟丁公，為項羽將，逐窘高祖彭城西。
sentences.literal: Bu's mother's younger brother Lord Ding, was Xiang Yu's general, pursued embarrassed Gaozu west of Pengcheng.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu's maternal uncle Lord Ding was a general under Xiang Yu and harried Gaozu into a tight corner west of Pengcheng.

sentences.chinese: 短兵接，漢王急，顧謂丁公曰：「兩賢豈相厄哉！」
sentences.literal: Short weapons met; King of Han urgent, turned told Lord Ding: "Two worthies how mutually press!"
sentences.idiomatic: At close quarters the King of Han was desperate; turning to Lord Ding he cried, "Surely two able men need not ruin each other!"

sentences.chinese: 丁公引兵而還。
sentences.literal: Lord Ding led troops and returned.
sentences.idiomatic: Lord Ding withdrew his troops and turned back.

sentences.chinese: 及項王滅，丁公謁見高祖，以丁公徇軍中，曰：「丁公為項王臣不忠，使項王失天下者也。」
sentences.literal: When King Xiang destroyed, Lord Ding audience-saw Gaozu; with Lord Ding displayed in army middle, said: "Lord Ding as King Xiang's minister not loyal—he who made King Xiang lose the realm."
sentences.idiomatic: After King Xiang fell, Lord Ding came to pay his respects to Gaozu; Gaozu had him paraded before the army and declared, "Lord Ding was a disloyal minister to King Xiang and was the man who cost him the empire."

sentences.chinese: 遂斬之，曰：「使後為人臣無傚丁公也！」
sentences.literal: Then beheaded him, said: "Make later those who are ministers not imitate Lord Ding!"
sentences.idiomatic: He had him executed, saying, "Let no minister hereafter take Lord Ding as his model!"

sentences.chinese: 欒布，梁人也。
sentences.literal: Luan Bu, Liang person.
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu was a native of Liang.

sentences.chinese: 彭越為家人時，嘗與布游，窮困，賣庸於齊，為酒家保。
sentences.literal: When Peng Yue was commoner, once with Bu roamed; poor trapped, sold hire-labor in Qi, was wine-house bondsman.
sentences.idiomatic: When Peng Yue was still a commoner, he had roamed with Luan Bu; in destitution he hired out his labor in Qi and worked as a bondservant in a tavern.

sentences.chinese: 數歲別去，而布為人所略，賣為奴於燕。
sentences.literal: Several years parted and left, while Bu by people was abducted, sold as slave in Yan.
sentences.idiomatic: After some years they parted, and Luan Bu was kidnapped and sold into slavery in Yan.

sentences.chinese: 為其主家報仇，燕將臧荼舉以為都尉。
sentences.literal: For his master's household avenged grudge; Yan general Zang Tu raised him to be commandant.
sentences.idiomatic: He avenged a wrong for his master's family, whereupon the Yan general Zang Tu promoted him to commandant.

sentences.chinese: 荼為燕王，布為將。
sentences.literal: Tu became Yan king; Bu was general.
sentences.idiomatic: When Zang Tu became King of Yan, Luan Bu served as his general.

sentences.chinese: 及荼反，漢擊燕，虜布。
sentences.literal: When Tu rebelled, Han struck Yan, captured Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: When Zang Tu rebelled, the Han attacked Yan and took Luan Bu prisoner.

sentences.chinese: 梁王彭越聞之，乃言上，請贖布為梁大夫。
sentences.literal: King of Liang Peng Yue heard it, then spoke to the sovereign, requested ransom Bu to be Liang grandee.
sentences.idiomatic: King Peng Yue of Liang heard of it and petitioned the emperor to ransom Luan Bu and appoint him a grandee of Liang.

sentences.chinese: 使於齊，未反，漢召彭越責以謀反，夷三族，梟首雒陽，下詔有收視者輒捕之。
sentences.literal: Sent on mission to Qi, not yet returned; Han summoned Peng Yue reproached with plotting rebellion, exterminated three clans, displayed head Luoyang, issued edict whoever collected viewed immediately arrest.
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu was on a mission to Qi and had not returned when the Han summoned Peng Yue, charged him with plotting rebellion, wiped out three clans of his kin, exposed his head in Luoyang, and issued an edict that anyone who dared mourn or bury him would be arrested at once.

sentences.chinese: 布還，奏事彭越頭下，祠而哭之。
sentences.literal: Bu returned, reported affairs beneath Peng Yue's head, sacrificed and wept for him.
sentences.idiomatic: When Luan Bu returned, he presented his report beneath Peng Yue's severed head, then offered sacrifice and wept for him.

sentences.chinese: 吏捕以聞。
sentences.literal: Officials arrested and reported.
sentences.idiomatic: The clerks seized him and reported the matter.

sentences.chinese: 上召布罵曰：「若與彭越反邪？
sentences.literal: The sovereign summoned Bu and cursed saying: "You with Peng Yue rebelled?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Luan Bu and shouted, "Were you in rebellion with Peng Yue?"

sentences.chinese: 吾禁人勿收，若獨祠而哭之，與反明矣。
sentences.literal: I forbade people not to collect; you alone sacrificed and wept for him—with rebel clear.
sentences.idiomatic: I forbade anyone to claim his body; you alone mourned him with sacrifice and tears—your guilt is as plain as if you had risen in arms."

sentences.chinese: 趣亨之。」
sentences.literal: Hurry boil him."
sentences.idiomatic: Take him away and boil him alive."

sentences.chinese: 方提趨湯，顧曰：「願一言而死。」
sentences.literal: Just as raised hurrying to cauldron, turned saying: "Wish one word then die."
sentences.idiomatic: As they were dragging him toward the cauldron, he looked back and said, "Let me speak one word before I die."

sentences.chinese: 上曰：「何言？」
sentences.literal: The sovereign said: "What words?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor said, "What is it?"

sentences.chinese: 布曰：「方上之困彭城，敗滎陽、成皋間，項王所以不能遂西，徒以彭王居梁地，與漢合從苦楚也。
sentences.literal: Bu said: "When the sovereign was trapped at Pengcheng, defeated between Xingyang and Chenggao, the reason King Xiang could not succeed west was only because Peng King dwelt in Liang territory, with Han joined vertical suffering Chu.
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu said, "When Your Majesty was pinned down at Pengcheng and driven from Xingyang to Chenggao, the reason King Xiang could not push west was solely that the King of Peng held Liang and allied with Han to exhaust Chu."

sentences.chinese: 當是之時，彭王壹顧，與楚則漢破，與漢則楚破。
sentences.literal: At this time, if Peng King one glance—with Chu then Han broken; with Han then Chu broken.
sentences.idiomatic: In those days a single shift of the King of Peng's weight—if he had thrown in with Chu, Han would have shattered; if with Han, Chu would have shattered."

sentences.chinese: 且垓下之會，微彭王，項氏不亡。
sentences.literal: Moreover at Gaixia's assembly, without Peng King, Xiang clan would not perish.
sentences.idiomatic: Moreover, at the battle of Gaixia, had it not been for the King of Peng, the house of Xiang would not have fallen."

sentences.chinese: 天下已定，彭王剖符受封，亦欲傳之萬世。
sentences.literal: The realm already settled, Peng King split tally received enfeoffment, also wished to transmit it ten thousand generations.
sentences.idiomatic: Once the realm was settled, the King of Peng received his tally and fief and hoped to hand them down for ages."

sentences.chinese: 今漢壹徵兵於梁，彭王病不行，而疑以為反。
sentences.literal: Now Han once called troops from Liang; Peng King sick did not go, yet suspected and deemed rebel.
sentences.idiomatic: Now the Han issued one levy in Liang; the king was ill and could not march, and on that slender ground he was suspected of treason."

sentences.chinese: 反形未見，以苛細誅之，臣恐功臣人人自危也。
sentences.literal: Rebel form not yet seen; by harsh fine executed him—your servant fears merit ministers each man self-will fear.
sentences.idiomatic: No sign of rebellion had appeared; to put him to death on petty charges makes me fear that every meritorious minister will begin to doubt his own safety."

sentences.chinese: 今彭王已死，臣生不如死，請就亨。」
sentences.literal: Now Peng King already dead; your servant alive not like dead; request approach boiling."
sentences.idiomatic: The King of Peng is already dead; I would rather die than live—send me to the cauldron."

sentences.chinese: 上乃釋布，拜為都尉。
sentences.literal: The sovereign then released Bu, appointed commandant.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor released him and appointed him commandant.

sentences.chinese: 孝文時，為燕相，至將軍。
sentences.literal: In Emperor Xiaowen's time, was Yan chancellor, reached general.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen he served as chancellor of Yan and rose to general.

sentences.chinese: 布稱曰：「窮困不能辱身，非人也；
sentences.literal: Bu declared saying: "Poor trapped yet cannot humble body—not a man;"
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu used to say, "To be down and out yet refuse to demean oneself is not to be human;"

sentences.chinese: 富貴不能快意，非賢也。」
sentences.literal: Wealth honor yet cannot satisfy will—not worthy."
sentences.idiomatic: to be rich and honored yet unable to follow one's conscience is not to be worthy."

sentences.chinese: 於是嘗有德，厚報之；
sentences.literal: Thus once had received virtue, thickly repaid it;
sentences.idiomatic: Accordingly, those who had once done him a kindness he repaid handsomely;

sentences.chinese: 有怨，必以法滅之。
sentences.literal: had grudge, surely by law destroyed them.
sentences.idiomatic: those who had wronged him he was sure to destroy within the law.

sentences.chinese: 吳楚反時，以功封為鄃侯，復為燕相。
sentences.literal: When Wu-Chu rebelled, by merit enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu, again was Yan chancellor.
sentences.idiomatic: During the rebellion of Wu and Chu he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu for his service and again became chancellor of Yan.

sentences.chinese: 燕齊之間皆為立社，號曰欒公社。
sentences.literal: Between Yan and Qi all for him established altars, called Luan Duke's altars.
sentences.idiomatic: Between Yan and Qi the people founded shrines to him and called them the altars of Duke Luan.

sentences.chinese: 布薨，子賁嗣侯，孝武時坐為太常犧牲不如令，國除。
sentences.literal: Bu died; son Ben succeeded marquis; in Emperor Xiaowu time sat crime as Grand Master of Ceremonies sacrificial victims not according order; state removed.
sentences.idiomatic: When Luan Bu died, his son Ben inherited the marquisate; under Emperor Wu he was convicted because sacrificial animals under his charge as Grand Master of Ceremonies failed to meet regulations, and the fief was abolished.

sentences.chinese: 田叔，趙陘城人也。
sentences.literal: Tian Shu, Zhao Xingcheng person.
sentences.idiomatic: Tian Shu was a native of Xingcheng in Zhao.

sentences.chinese: 其先，齊田氏也。
sentences.literal: His ancestors, Qi Tian clan.
sentences.idiomatic: His ancestors were the Tian clan of Qi.

sentences.chinese: 叔好劍，學黃老術於樂鉅公。
sentences.literal: Shu liked sword; studied Huang-Lao arts with Yue Ju-gong.
sentences.idiomatic: Tian Shu loved the sword and studied the teachings of Huangdi and Laozi under Master Yue Ju.

sentences.chinese: 為人廉直，喜任俠。
sentences.literal: As man incorrupt straight, delighted in knight-errantry.
sentences.idiomatic: He was incorruptible and upright by nature and delighted in knight-errantry.

sentences.chinese: 游諸公，趙人舉之趙相趙午，言之趙王張敖，以為郎中。
sentences.literal: Roamed the great lords; Zhao men raised him to Zhao chancellor Zhao Wu, spoke him to Zhao King Zhang Ao, made Gentleman of the Palace.
sentences.idiomatic: He moved among the great houses until men of Zhao recommended him to the Zhao chancellor Zhao Wu, who spoke of him to King Zhang Ao of Zhao, who appointed him Gentleman of the Palace.

sentences.chinese: 數歲，趙王賢之，未及遷。
sentences.literal: Several years, Zhao king deemed him worthy, not yet reached promotion.
sentences.idiomatic: For several years the king of Zhao admired him but had not yet promoted him.

sentences.chinese: 會趙午、貫高等謀弒上，事發覺，漢下詔捕趙王及群臣反者。
sentences.literal: It happened Zhao Wu, Guan Gao and others plotted assassinate the sovereign; affair discovered; Han issued edict arrest Zhao king and ministers who rebelled.
sentences.idiomatic: It happened that Zhao Wu, Guan Gao, and others plotted to murder the emperor; when the plot was uncovered, the Han issued an edict to seize the king of Zhao and all ministers involved in the conspiracy.

sentences.chinese: 趙有敢隨王，罪三族。
sentences.literal: Zhao had whoever dared follow king, crime three clans.
sentences.idiomatic: In Zhao, anyone who dared follow the king would suffer extermination of three clans.
